Exploration of Water Springs L1V 511
If a Karnikara t'Barahal) or Pala^a {Butea Frondosd) tree bears
white flowers (instead of red ones), there will be water two cubits to
its south at a depth of 10 cubits.

If steam or smoke arises from the earth, there will be water
underneath at 10 cubits; and it should be stated that the vein will
yield an abundant supply of water.
gn H^rftRT \\%iu
If in the region of a field the crops that have grown perish or
if the vegetation there is very glossy and exceedingly white, there
will be a vein of abundant water on the spot at a depth of 10 cubits.
[The above exposition relates to the avaiiability of water in
jungle and watery regions. Hereafter the author treats of water-
divination in desert areas.]
